meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build SaaS pricing models to overcome no portfolio. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for professional results.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-fidelity SaaS pricing engine. This demonstrates architectural expertise, database management, and business logic. By delivering a production-ready pricing component, you move from 'novice developer' to 'strategic partner,' proving to clients you can handle the complex revenue mechanics that drive their business growth.
content.intro:
You’re staring at an empty GitHub profile, feeling the cold sting of client rejection. The market doesn't care about your potential; it cares about your output. Every day you spend without a portfolio is a day you lose high-ticket contracts to someone who simply looked more capable.

The secret isn't building a generic to-do app. It’s building the boring, high-stakes infrastructure that businesses actually pay for: a dynamic SaaS pricing engine. When you show a client a robust, scalable pricing component, you stop being a freelancer and start being a business asset.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema that supports tiered pricing, billing cycles, and feature-flagging logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS pricing table including tiers (free, pro, enterprise), monthly/yearly prices, and a JSONB column for feature sets.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file schema.sql --context 'PostgreSQL schema for SaaS pricing with JSONB features'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Logic Engine
steps.description: Write the core business logic that calculates totals based on user selection and cycle frequency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript function that calculates subscription costs, factoring in prorated upgrades and annual discount logic.

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your engine to a payment processor to ensure your portfolio piece is functional, not just theoretical.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route handler using Stripe SDK to create a Checkout Session based on the chosen pricing tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --package stripe --context 'Implement Stripe Checkout session creation'

steps.title: Design a Responsive Pricing Table
steps.description: Create a high-conversion UI component that displays the pricing tiers dynamically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Tailwind CSS pricing table component that highlights the 'Pro' plan and fetches data from the pricing API.

steps.title: Automate Unit Testing
steps.description: Validate your pricing logic with automated tests to show clients you prioritize reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the pricing logic ensuring edge cases like annual discounts and trial periods are handled.

pro_tips: Always store prices in cents as integers in the database to avoid floating-point math errors.
Include a 'Contact Us' CTA for enterprise tiers to show you understand B2B lead generation.
Use mock data that mirrors real-world subscription churn scenarios to demonstrate advanced logic.
Add a toggle for Monthly vs. Yearly view; it is a standard expectation in enterprise SaaS.
Document your API endpoints with Swagger/OpenAPI to signal professional engineering standards.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to Vercel or Netlify, add it to your personal portfolio site, and record a 60-second Loom walkthrough explaining the architecture to share on LinkedIn.
content.conversion_block: Stop building toys and start building assets that close deals. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
